events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need decide compliance for transformers dry-type & pad-mount industrial site, observed primary/secondary landed reversed, rough-complete. Need apply NEC Art. 450, 250.30. Primary/secondary reversed is serious: transformer primary and secondary conductors swapped. This can cause overvoltage on secondary side, damage equipment, safety hazard, violates NEC 110.3(B) installation per listing/manufacturer, Art. 450 transformer requirements, 250.30 grounding separately derived systems. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Reversed primary/secondary can energize secondary at primary voltage, potential arc flash, equipment damage, injury/death. In industrial site heavy, critical maybe. But maybe high. Need choose. Component conditions installed-defective and non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80. value not asked 0. sound_source etc default empty? Schema says string for sound_source, numbers. Use empty string and 0. order empty list, workable empty list. findings: primary and secondary conductors landed reversed; transformer not installed per manufacturer/ NEC; potential overvoltage/energization hazard; grounding/bonding of separately derived system may be incorrect. actions: de-energize transformer; verify nameplate primary/secondary terminals; re-land conductors per manufacturer wiring diagram; perform polarity/phase rotation and ratio tests; verify grounding per 250.30; re-inspect before energization. rationale: Reversing primary and secondary violates NEC 110.3(B), Art. 450, and manufacturer instructions, creating overvoltage and shock/fire hazard; must be corrected. references: NEC 110.3(B), NEC Art. 450, NEC 250.30. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Primary and secondary conductors are landed reversed on the transformer terminals.",
    "Transformer is not installed per the manufacturer’s wiring diagram and nameplate ratings.",
    "Reversed primary/secondary can energize the secondary winding at primary voltage, creating severe overvoltage, equipment damage, arc-flash, shock, and fire hazards.",
    "Grounding/bonding of the separately derived system may not be correct because of the reversed terminal landing."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize and lock out/tag out the transformer and associated feeders before any correction.",
    "Verify transformer nameplate primary/secondary voltage, terminal designations, and phasing against the approved drawings.",
    "Remove and re-land primary and secondary conductors on the correct terminals per the manufacturer’s instructions and project drawings.",
    "Confirm proper grounding and bonding of the separately derived system per NEC 250.30.",
    "Perform turns-ratio, polarity, continuity, insulation-resistance, and phase-rotation tests before re-energization.",
    "Re-inspect and obtain approval from a qualified electrician or inspector before closing out or energizing."
  ],
  "rationale": "Landing the primary and secondary conductors in reverse violates NEC 110.3(B), Article 450 transformer installation requirements, and the manufacturer’s wiring instructions. It creates a critical shock, fire, and equipment-damage hazard because the secondary system can be energized at primary voltage. The defect must be corrected and verified before the transformer is placed in service.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.3(B)",
    "NEC Article 450",
    "NEC 250.30"
  ]
}
